document.docAuthor: López, Tatiana Cárdenas, Flowers, Ralph Wills & Dias, Lucimar Gomes
document.docTitle: Tricorythodes carare López & Flowers & Dias 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: A new species of Tricorythodes Ulmer from Colombia and additional notes on T serratus (Baumgardner & Ávila) (Ephemeroptera: Leptohyphidae)

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: A new species of Tricorythodes Ulmer from Colombia and additional notes on T serratus (Baumgardner & Ávila) (Ephemeroptera: Leptohyphidae)

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Universidad de Caldas, Facultad de Ciencias Exactas y Naturales, Manizales, Colombia.
Florida A & M University, Tallahassee, Florida, United States.
Universidad de Caldas, Departamento de Ciencias Biológicas, Facultad de Ciencias Exactas y Naturales, Grupo de investigación BIONAT, Manizales, Colombia.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Material examined.
HOLOTYPE : 1 male nymph, COLOMBIA , Caldas department , Norcasia municipality, Reserva Natural del Río Manso , 5°39’51.3’’N , 74°46’56.6’’W ,
160m
,
February 2012
, collectors: Lucimar Gomes Dias and Yeisson Gutierrez.
ALLOTYPE : 1 female nymph, same data as holotype
.
PARATYPE : 1 male nymph, same data as holotype
.
4 female nymphs, same data as holotype, except:
November 2014
and collectors: Lucimar Gomes Dias and Tatiana Cárdenas López.
2 female nymphs, COLOMBIA , Tolima department , Coello municipality, Río Coello , 4°17′18″N 74°53′52″W ,
300m
,
July 2012
, collector: Ana Meza. The type material is deposited in the “Colección Entomológica del Programa de Biología de la Universidad de Caldas ”, Colombia ( CEBUC )
.

caption.paragraph: TABLE 2 . Pairwise nucleotide divergence matrix estimated using the Kimura 2-parameter based on partial COI gene.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Diagnosis .
Tricorythodes carare
differs from the others species by the following combination of characteristics: maxillary palp 3-segmented, with apical setae ( Fig. 4E and 7B ); dorsal surface of median and hind femora with a longitudinal row of 5 to 9 chalazae serrated in the apex ( Figs. 5C–D ); tarsal claws with 4 or 5 marginal denticles and with 1–3 submarginal denticles near apex ( Figs. 5A and 6B–D ) and median and hind tarsal claws with one submarginal denticle ( Figs. 6E–F ); posterior margin of the abdominal terga with chalazae serrated in the apex ( Figs. 7C–D ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 3. Habitus
T. carare
sp. nov.
A, Male; B, Female.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Holotype mature male nymph:
Length of male, 5.0– 5.4 mm ; body (without filaments), 2.9–3.3 mm ; caudal filaments, 2.0–3.0 mm; length of female, 5.9–6.8 mm ; body (without filaments), 3.2–3.5 mm ; caudal filaments, 2.6–3.3 mm (n=6). General coloration whitish, with diffuse gray ( Fig. 3A ). Head. Genal projection little developed. Head whitish with occipital region shaded with gray ( Figs. 3A ). Antennae translucent. Mouthparts yellowish ( Fig. 4 ); maxillary palp 3-segmented with an apical seta ( Fig. 4E and 7B ). Thorax. General coloration whitish shaded gray ( Figs. 3A ). Pronotum shaded with gray, except by the lateral margin of pronotum not pigmented; anterior margin of pronotum with simple, thin, scattered setae. Mesonotum whitish shaded with gray, except midline and the inner margin of the wing pads; wing pads whitish, shaded with gray, with veins and external margin dark gray. Metanotum whitish and shaded with gray. Legs ( Figs. 5B–D and 6A ): general coloration pale with diffuse gray. Femora relatively wide with total length/ maximum width ratio forelegs = 1.5–1.7 and mid and hind legs = 1.9–2.2. Fore femur with a submedian transverse row of 6 to 13 chalazae, and with a longitudinal row of 2 to 5 chalazae, inner margin with acuminate and simple setae, outer margin with chalazae and acuminate setae ( Fig. 5B ). All chalazae present serrated apex. Middle and hind femora with a transverse row of 4 to 8 chalazae and acuminate setae in the basal region; dorsal surface with a longitudinal row with 5 to 9 chalazae; inner and outer margin with chalazae and acuminate setae ( Figs. 5C–D ). Tibia: pale with a darker gray band in the middle; dorsal surface with a longitudinal row of 4 to 6 acuminate setae ( Figs. 5B–D and 6A ), apex with branched pectinate setae; inner and outer margins with simple and acuminate setae. Tarsi: pale coloration, with a gray band in the middle; apex with branched setae; inner and outer margins with simple setae ( Figs. 5B–D ). Claw ( Figs. 5A and 6B–F ): pale yellow coloration, with dark apex; 4 to 6 marginal denticles; apex with a small and simple seta ( Figs. 5A and 6C ). Fore claw: with 1–3 subapical denticles ( Figs. 5A and 6B–D ). Middle and hind claw: with one subapical denticle ( Figs. 6E–F ). Thoracic pleurae and sterna pale shaded lightly with grey. Abdomen. General coloration whitish shaded with gray, except on lateral margins and midline of the tergites VII–IX ( Figs. 3A–B ). Dorsal surface of the terga with small chalazae and numerous small setae ( Fig. 7C– F ). Posterior margins of abdominal terga with numerous spines and small chalazae ( Figs. 7C–D ). Tergum I elevated in middle region, tergum II– VI with less marked median elevation. Terga II–IX laterally expanded, with posterolateral projections present on segments VI –IX ( Figs. 3A–B ). Abdominal sterna pale, with slight grayish tinge, with a dark submedian longitudinal band in sterna I–VIII. Opercular gill subtriangular, pale, with extensive diffuse gray marks. Caudal filaments pale with dark yellow base, posterior margin of each joint with simple and acuminate setae.
Note. Some specimens are shaded with reddish instead of gray ( Fig. 3B ).
Etymology. The epithet is a noun in apposition. Opon-Carare is an indigenous language that was spoken in the Mediterranean Magdalena Valley until the 20th century.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 4.
T. carare
sp. nov.
, mouthparts. A, Labrum detail, d.v.; B, Right mandible, d.v.; C, Left mandible, d.v.; D, Maxillary palp; E, Maxillary palp, detail; F, Labium, v.v.

subSubSection.paragraph:
Other material examined (this material corresponds to undescribed species
T. aff. serratus
): 2 male nymphs and
2 female nymphs, COLOMBIA , Magdalena department , Parque Natural Nacional Tayrona , Santa Marta municipality, Quebrada Mason , 11°17’36.2’’ N , 73°56’08.5’’ W ,
195m
,
September 2015
, collector: Cristian Granados.
1 male nymph, COLOMBIA , Sucre department , Colosó municipality, Arroyo el Bobo , 9°33’48.4”N , 75°24’42.8”W ,
47m
,
November 2015
, collector: Eliana Contreras Martinez.
1 male nymph, COLOMBIA , San Onofre municipality, Malagana-San Onofre , Arroyo Corte Alto , 9°50’29.6”N , 75°25’0.6”W ,
41m
,
November 2015
, collector: Eliana Contreras Martinez.
3 male nymphs, COLOMBIA , Chocó department , Acandí municipality, Serranía de Tripogadí , Quebrada Coquital , 08°23’58” N , 77°08’46” W ,
115m
,
June 2000
, collector: María Paula Rozo.
1 male nymph and
1 female , ECUADOR , Esmeraldas province , Quinindé , Río Ducha , 0º44’23.8”N 79º11’59.9”W ,
300 m
,
November 2009
. Collectors : Sandra Pérez and Wills Flowers.
